task_completion.visual_evidence: The OpenToonz main window is visible on the screen.

task_completion.description: The correct source scene is loaded in OpenToonz.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The OpenToonz interface shows 'dwanko_run' in the title bar, xsheet, or scene viewer, indicating the scene is loaded.

task_completion.description: The export or save dialog is configured with the correct output directory.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A file dialog (like Export Scene or Save As) is open in OpenToonz, with the destination path set to or navigating to '/home/ga/OpenToonz/output/handoff_package'.

task_completion.description: The scene and its drawing assets are successfully exported to the destination folder.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A file manager or terminal window shows 'dwanko_run.tnz' and associated asset files (specifically '.pli' files like raylit_source.pli or shadow.pli) present inside the '/home/ga/OpenToonz/output/handoff_package' directory or its subdirectories.

integrity.description: The agent used the OpenToonz GUI to perform the export.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show interaction with OpenToonz menus and dialogs for exporting/saving, rather than terminal commands like 'cp' doing the work.

integrity.description: The agent interacted with the actual OpenToonz application.
integrity.visual_evidence: The OpenToonz graphical interface is clearly visible and used, not just a text editor or script.

integrity.description: The agent did not manually edit the scene file to fake the export.
integrity.visual_evidence: There are no text editors (like nano, vim, gedit) open showing the XML contents of the .tnz file being manually modified to change file paths.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The source scene is located at /home/ga/OpenToonz/samples/dwanko_run.tnz. The required output directory is /home/ga/OpenToonz/output/handoff_package. The expected scene name is dwanko_run.tnz. The scene natively references .pli files (e.g., raylit_source.pli, shadow.pli), which must be present in the output directory to confirm assets were properly copied and the package is self-contained.
